videoTitle: Lesson 1.3- Core Skills You Need to Become A Successful Marketing Automation Specialist.mp4
transcript: Welcome back. So far, we've covered what a marketing automation specialist is and what your responsibilities will be. Now, it's time to talk about the skills you need to actually do this work effectively. In this lesson, I'm going to break down the seven core skills that every successful marketing automation specialist must develop. These aren't just theoretical concepts. These are the practical abilities that will make you valuable, help you land clients, and allow you to deliver real results. The good news, every single one of these skills can be learned and that's exactly what we're going to do in this course. Let's dive in. There are seven core skills we're going to master together. Understanding business and client requirements, understanding marketing campaigns inbound versus outbound, funnels and funnel design, creating high converting landing pages, creating effective email and SMS campaigns, building workflows and automations, analytics and optimization. Each of these skills has its own dedicated module in this course, but let me give you an overview of what each one involves and why it matters. Skill number one, understanding business and client requirements. This is where everything starts. Before you can even build any automation, you need to deeply understand what does the business sell? Its products, services, and offers. Who are they selling to? Your target audience or customer personas. What is the customer journey? How do people discover and consider and then buy? What are the business goals? Is it more leads or more sales or maybe better retention? What problems need solving? Where is the current marketing breaking down? Why does this matter? Because if you don't understand the business, you'll build automations that don't actually help. You might create something technically impressive that completely misses the mark. The best marketing automation specialists are great listeners and ask powerful questions. In module two, we'll teach you exactly how to conduct discovery calls, create customer personas, map customer journeys, and translate business needs into automation strategies. Skill number two, understanding marketing campaigns, inbound versus outbound. Marketing automation supports both inbound and outbound marketing, and you need to understand the difference. Inbound marketing is about attracting customers to you through valuable content like SEO, social media, and lead magnets. Think of it as fishing with bait. You create something valuable and customers come to you. Outbound marketing is about reaching out to prospects through ads, called emails, called calls, and direct outreach. Think of it as fishing with a net. You go out and find customers. Most businesses use a combination of both, and your automations need to support whatever strategy they are using. You need to know when to use inbound versus outbound approaches, how to design campaigns for each, how to integrate them for maximum impact what automation workflows work best for each type In module 3 we break down both approaches and show you how to build automations that support each strategy Skill number 3 funnels and funnel design. A marketing funnel is the path that takes someone from I've never heard of this business to I'm a paying customer. Understanding funnels means knowing the stages of a funnel like awareness, consideration, or decision. Different types of funnels. It can be a lead generation funnel, sales funnel, webinar, or an application funnel. How to map out each step of the customer journey. Where automation fits in each stage. And how to identify and fix bottlenecks. In this course, you'll learn to use a tool called Miro to visually design and map out funnels. This is crucial because before you build anything in a platform like GoHighLevel, you need a clear blueprint. Imagine trying to build a house without architectural plans. That's what building automation without a funnel map is like. Module 4 is entirely dedicated to funnel design, and you'll come out of it able to create professional funnel diagrams that clients can immediately understand. Skill number four, creating high-converting landing pages. A landing page is where your funnel typically begins. It's where visitors arrive and decide whether to take action or leave. Creating high-converting landing pages requires understanding the anatomy of an effective landing page, writing compelling headlines and copy, designing clear calls to action, Using images, videos, and social proof effectively Building pages in platforms like GoHighLevel Optimizing for mobile devices And A-B testing to improve performance A mediocre landing page might convert at 2% A great one might convert at 20% or higher That's the difference between struggling and thriving for most businesses In Module 5, we'll teach you the exact framework for building landing pages that convert. And you'll practice building real pages in Go High Level. Skill number 5, creating effective email and SMS campaigns. Email and SMS are the lifeblood of marketing automation. You need to master email marketing fundamentals like deliverability, open rates, and click rates. Different types of campaigns like welcome, nurture, promotional, or re-engagement Writing email copy that gets clicks and conversions Email design and layout best practices SMS marketing and compliance Regulations like TCPA and GDPR Segmentation and personalization strategies And how to analyze campaign performance A well-crafted email sequence can nurture a cold lead into a hot prospect ready to buy. A poorly written one gets ignored or marked as a spam. Module 6 will teach you the psychology of effective messaging and the technical skills to build campaigns in Go High Level that actually get results. Skill number 6. Building workflows and automations via Go High Level. This is the technical heart of marketing automation. You need to learn how to use GoHighLevel the platform that we be focusing on understanding triggers actions and conditions building workflows that respond to user behavior creating nurture sequences appointment reminders and follow pipeline and tag management multi automation like email plus SMS testing and troubleshooting workflows and integrating with other tools via webhooks and APIs Think of this as learning to code but for marketing You're creating logical sequences that make things happen automatically based on specific conditions. Don't worry if this sounds technical. We'll start simple and build up. By the end of Module 7, you'll be confidently building complex automation workflows. Skill number seven, analytics and optimization. Building automations is only half the job. You also need to measure performance and continuously improve. This means knowing which metrics matter, like conversion rate, ROI, CAC, LTV, setting up tracking and analytics correctly, reading and interpreting campaign reports, using tools like Google Analytics and UTM parameters, running A-B tests to improve results, applying conversion rate optimization techniques, creating client-facing performance reports, and making data-driven decisions. Clients don't just want automations. They want results. Your ability to prove ROI and continuously improve performance is what turns one-time projects into long-term retainers. Module 8 will teach you how to become a data savvy and use analytics to optimize everything you build. Now here's what's important to understand. These seven skills don't work in isolation. They all connect and support each other. Let me show you how this works with a real example. Imagine a client comes to you. They're a business coach who wants more discovery calls booked. Step 1. You use your business requirement skills to understand their offers, audience, and customer journey. Step 2. You determine they need an inbound strategy with a lead magnet to attract potential clients. Step 3. You design a funnel that takes people from a Facebook ad to a landing page through an email sequence to a book call. Step four, you build a high-converting landing page that offers a free guide in exchange for a contact info. Step five, you create an email sequence that nurtures leads and positions the coach as an expert. Step six, you build the automation workflow in GoHighLevel that delivers the guide, sends the emails, and books calls. In Step 7, you track the performance, A-B test subject lines, and optimize the funnel to increase booking rates. See how it all connects? Each skill supports the others to create a complete, effective marketing automation system. In this course, we're going to develop each of these skills step-by-step. Modules 2 to 8 each focus on one core skill. You'll get theory, examples, and hands-on practice. You'll build real projects you can add to your portfolio. By module 9, you'll put it all together in a capstone project. This isn't just about watching videos. You'll be actively building and creating throughout the course. Here's something important. You don't need to be a master of all seven skills before you can start getting clients. As you develop these skills, you'll become more valuable. But even with an intermediate skill across the board, you can deliver tremendous value to small businesses who currently have no automation at all. The key is to be honest about your skill level. Start with projects that match your abilities and continuously improve. I also want to mention that there are some soft skills that will make you even more successful like communication, explaining technical concepts in simple terms? Problem solving, figuring out solutions when things don work as expected Attention to detail catching small errors before they become big problems Time management Juggling multiple projects and deadlines Client management Setting expectations and maintaining good relationships. You will develop these naturally as you work through projects, but it's good to be aware of them. Let me paint a picture of what makes someone truly successful in this role. It's not just about knowing how to use Go High Level. It's about understanding what businesses need, thinking strategically about marketing, building systems that actually work, measuring and providing results, communicating value to clients, continuously learning and adapting. The best specialists combine technical skills with strategic thinking and professional excellence. Here's the exciting part. By mastering these seven core skills, you'll have a significant competitive advantage. Most people in this space have partial skills. They might know how to send emails but not design funnels. Or they can build workflows but can't create landing pages. You're going to have the complete package. That makes you incredibly valuable. Now, I want to be realistic about what's required from you. Time. Expect 8 to 12 weeks to complete the course if you're dedicated, longer if you're part-time. Effort. This requires active participation, not passive watching. You need to build things. Mindset. Come with a willingness to learn, make mistakes, and grow. Resources. You will need a computer and internet. Access to go high level for practice is helpful, but we'll show you how to get started. And here's what you'll get in return. A skill set that can earn you 50,000 pesos to 150,000 plus per month, and the ability to work from anywhere, and the career that gives you freedom and flexibility. So there you have it, the seven core skills you need to become a successful marketing automation specialist. Understanding business and client requirements, marketing campaigns, inbound versus outbound, funnels and funnel design, high converting landing pages, email and SMS campaigns, workflows and automations, analytics and optimization. Each one is learnable. Each one is valuable. And together, they make you an incredibly marketable professional. Starting with the next module, we're going to dive deep into each of these skills. We'll start with module two, understanding business and client requirements because that's where every successful project begins. You're going to learn exactly how to conduct discovery calls, ask the right questions, understand what clients really need, and position yourself as a trusted expert from day one. I'm excited to continue this journey with you. You've completed module one which means you now understand what this role is, what you'll be doing, and what skills you need to develop. Now it's time to start building those skills. Let's keep going. See you in Module 2. Thank you for watching!

visualDescriptions.description:
The image presents a screenshot of an online course or presentation slide, divided into two sections. The left side features a dark background with white text, while the right side has a light-colored background with a list of topics.

**Left Section:**

* **Title:** "Today's Goal: Master the 7 Core Skills" in large white text
* **Subtitle:** "These aren't just theoretical concepts—these are the practical, learnable abilities that will make you valuable and allow you to deliver real results." in smaller white text
* **Text Box:**
	+ **Header:** "WHAT YOU'LL LEARN" in white text on a dark background
	+ **Description:** "We'll break down each skill, why it matters, and how you'll master it in this course." in white text on a dark background

**Right Section:**

* **List of Topics:**
	1. Understanding Business Requirements
	2. Marketing Campaigns (Inbound/Outbound)
	3. Funnels & Funnel Design
	4. High-Converting Landing Pages
	5. Email & SMS Campaigns
	6. Workflows & Automations
	7. Analytics & Optimization

The image effectively conveys the main objective of the course, which is to teach seven core skills essential for delivering real results. The list of topics on the right side provides a clear outline of what will be covered in the course. Overall, the image presents a concise and organized overview of the course content.

The image shows a man sitting in front of a white wall with guitars hanging on it, speaking to the camera.

*   The man is wearing a tan t-shirt and has short black hair. 
*   He appears to be speaking or presenting to the camera, as indicated by his open mouth and gesturing hands.
*   There is a black microphone in front of him, partially visible under his shirt.
*   Behind him, there are two guitars hanging on the wall: one is dark gray, and the other is light brown.
*   To the right of the man, there is a bed with a white comforter and a black metal headboard. 

The overall atmosphere suggests that the man is recording a video or podcast in his home.

The image presents a comprehensive flowchart illustrating the integration of seven core skills to solve a real-world client problem. The title, "All Skills Working Together," is prominently displayed at the top, accompanied by the subtitle "How the 7 core skills integrate to solve a real-world client problem." The flowchart is divided into eight distinct sections, each representing a crucial step in the process.

*   **The Request**
    *   The client's request is presented in a gray box on the left side of the image, stating, "I need more qualified discovery calls booked automatically."
*   **Requirements: Discovery**
    *   This section involves analyzing offers, target audience personas, and current gaps.
*   **Campaigns: Strategy**
    *   Determine the inbound strategy using a high-value Lead Magnet.
*   **Funnels: Design**
    *   Map the flow: Ad → Opt-in → Email → Calendar Booking.
*   **Landing Pages: Build Assets**
    *   Create a high-converting opt-in page and thank you page.
*   **Email/SMS: Nurture**
    *   Write an email sequence to build trust and position expertise.
*   **Workflows: Automation**
    *   Build the engine in GHL to deliver the guide and book calls.
*   **Analytics: Optimize**
    *   Track booking rates, A/B test headlines, and refine the funnel.
*   **The Outcome: Success**
    *   Automated system generating qualified calls 24/7.

The flowchart effectively illustrates how these seven core skills work together to address the client's request, ultimately leading to a successful outcome. By following this structured approach, businesses can streamline their processes and achieve their goals more efficiently.

The image presents a graph illustrating the skill progression journey from beginner to master, with a focus on the idea that one doesn't need to be 100% proficient to start. The graph is titled "You Don't Need 100% to Start" and features a green line representing the skill level over time.

*   **Title and Subtitle**
    *   The title "You Don't Need 100% to Start" is prominently displayed at the top of the image.
    *   The subtitle "Skill progression journey from beginner to master" provides context for the graph.
*   **Graph**
    *   The graph shows a green line representing the skill level over time, with a dotted orange line indicating the client-ready threshold.
    *   The y-axis represents the skill proficiency percentage, ranging from 0% (beginner) to 100% (master).
    *   The x-axis represents time, with labels for months 1-6 and an "ongoing" category.
    *   The graph highlights key milestones, including:
        *   **Client-Ready**: A yellow box notes that one can start taking paid clients once they reach this threshold, which appears to be around 60% skill proficiency.
        *   **Mastery Journey**: A green box emphasizes that mastery is a continuous improvement journey over time, not a destination.
*   **Key Insight**
    *   A light green box at the bottom of the image provides a key insight: most businesses have zero automation, and even at a 60% skill level, one can provide massive value compared to their current manual processes.

In summary, the image effectively conveys that one can start taking on clients and providing value even before reaching 100% proficiency, with a focus on continuous improvement and the importance of automation in business.

The image presents a comprehensive overview of the essential skills required for professional excellence, focusing on three key areas: Technical Skills, Strategic Thinking, and Professional Excellence. The infographic is structured around a central figure representing an individual, with each skill set branching out from this figure to emphasize their interconnectedness.

* **Technical Skills**
	+ Knows the tools inside-out
	+ Builds clean, logical workflows
	+ Understands data & metrics
	+ Stays updated on features
* **Strategic Thinking**
	+ Understands business goals
	+ Thinks like a marketer
	+ Solves real problems
	+ Delivers measurable ROI
* **Professional Excellence**
	+ Communicates clearly
	+ Continuously improves
	+ Manages projects well
	+ Client-focused mindset

The infographic effectively illustrates the importance of balancing technical proficiency, strategic vision, and professional conduct to achieve overall excellence in one's career or profession. By highlighting these interconnected skill sets, the image provides a clear roadmap for individuals seeking to enhance their capabilities and succeed in their field.

The image presents a concise overview of the necessary investments required to master marketing automation, divided into four key categories: Time, Effort, Mindset, and Resources.

*   **Time**
    *   The image indicates that completing the full course will take 8-12 weeks.
    *   A daily study time of 2-3 hours is recommended.
    *   It emphasizes the importance of a lifetime of continuous learning and updates.
*   **Effort**
    *   Active participation (not passive watching) is required.
    *   Hands-on practice with automation tools is necessary.
    *   Building real projects from scratch is essential.
    *   Pushing through technical challenges is also important.
*   **Mindset**
    *   A beginner's mindset: Willingness to learn
    *   Growth mindset: Embrace mistakes
    *   Professional mindset: Deliver value
    *   Patient mindset: Skills take time
*   **Resources**
    *   Computer with reliable internet access
    *   GHL trial/account for hands-on practice
    *   Note-taking tools for documentation
    *   Optional but helpful

In summary, the image provides a clear and structured outline of what is required to invest in mastering marketing automation, covering time commitment, effort, mindset, and resources. By understanding these requirements, individuals can better prepare themselves for the challenges and opportunities that come with learning marketing automation.

The image presents a comprehensive comparison between Inbound and Outbound Marketing strategies, highlighting their distinct approaches and techniques.

* **Title**: 
	+ The title of the image is "Inbound vs. Outbound Marketing"
* **Inbound Marketing**
	+ **Definition**: They come to you
	+ **Techniques**:
		- Content Marketing
		- SEO & Organic Search
		- Social Media Engagement
		- Lead Magnets
* **Outbound Marketing**
	+ **Definition**: You go to them
	+ **Techniques**:
		- Paid Advertisements
		- Cold Email Outreach
		- Cold Calling
		- Direct Outreach / DM
* **Integrated Strategy**
	+ Most businesses need BOTH

In summary, the image effectively illustrates the fundamental differences between Inbound and Outbound Marketing strategies, providing a clear understanding of each approach and its respective techniques. By highlighting the distinct definitions and methods of each strategy, the image emphasizes the importance of an integrated approach, suggesting that most businesses require a combination of both Inbound and Outbound Marketing to achieve optimal results.

The image displays a marketing funnel diagram with three stages: **Awareness**, **Consideration**, and **Decision**. 

**Funnel Stages:**
* **Awareness (Top of Funnel - TOFU)**: Cold traffic, never heard of you
* **Consideration (Middle of Funnel - MOFU)**: Researching, comparing options
* **Decision (Bottom of Funnel - BOFU)**: Ready to buy, evaluating offer

The funnel's goal is to guide potential customers through these stages, culminating in a **Customer Purchased!** outcome.

**Additional Information:**
The right side of the image provides supplementary information on:
* **The Customer Journey Map**: 
  * Map the Journey
  * Fit Automations
  * Find Bottlenecks

The image presents a comprehensive guide to creating high-converting landing pages, titled "Skill #4: High-Converting Landing Pages" in bold black text at the top. The subtitle, "The anatomy of pages that convert," is written in smaller green text below.

**Visual Representation:**

On the left side of the image, a graphic illustrates the anatomy of a landing page, featuring a browser window with a white background and a green-outlined rectangle indicating the page's content area. The page is divided into four sections, each marked with a green numbered circle:

* A dark bar at the top
* A light blue bar below
* A green button
* A large light blue rectangle

**Key Elements:**

On the right side of the image, five key elements are listed in green text, accompanied by brief descriptions:

* **Compelling Headline**: Hooks attention immediately with clear value proposition
* **Benefit-Focused Copy**: Focuses on user outcomes, not just features
* **Clear Call-to-Action**: Single, prominent goal (no distractions)
* **Trust Elements**: Social proof, testimonials, and security badges

**Additional Features:**

At the bottom of the image, three additional features are highlighted:

* **Mobile Responsive**
* **Fast Loading**
* **A/B Optimized**

These features are presented in small white boxes with green icons, emphasizing their importance for high-converting landing pages. Overall, the image provides a clear and concise guide to creating effective landing pages that drive conversions.

The image presents a comprehensive overview of various campaign types that can be mastered, with a focus on email workflows and SMS marketing. The title "Campaign Types You'll Master" is prominently displayed at the top.

**Core Email Workflows**

* **Welcome Series**
	+ Make a great first impression
	+ 60%+ open rates
* **Nurture Campaigns**
	+ Build trust and relationships over time
* **Promotional**
	+ Drive sales with compelling offers
* **Re-engagement**
	+ Win back cold leads

**SMS**

* A sample SMS message on a phone screen:
	+ "Hi John, checking in on your project..."
	+ "URGENT: Your offer expires in 1 hour!" 

**Key Takeaways**

* Segmentation
* Deliverability
* Compliance

The image effectively communicates the different types of campaigns that can be mastered, along with their goals and benefits. The use of concise language and visually appealing graphics makes it easy to understand and navigate. Overall, the image provides a clear and comprehensive overview of the various campaign types, making it a valuable resource for those looking to improve their marketing strategies.

The image presents a screenshot of a marketing performance dashboard, titled **Key Metrics Dashboard** in bold black text at the top. The dashboard is divided into four main sections: **Email Performance**, **Revenue Metrics**, **Cost Efficiency**, and **Funnel Health**, each providing key metrics and statistics.

* **Email Performance**
	+ Open Rate: **35.0%** (↑ 2.1%)
	+ Click Rate: **8.5%** (↑ 0.8%)
	+ Unsubscribe: **0.3%** (Healthy)
* **Revenue Metrics**
	+ Conversion Rate: **4.2%** (↑ 0.5%)
	+ Avg Order Value: **$247** (Flat)
	+ Total Revenue: **$45.2k** (↑ 12%)
* **Cost Efficiency**
	+ Cost Per Lead: **$12.00** (Optimized)
	+ CPA: **$89.00** (Target: $90)
	+ ROI: **278%** (Excellent)
* **Funnel Health**
	+ Landing Page: **18.0%** (Visitor to Lead)
	+ Lead to Customer: **23.0%** (Sales Conv.)
	+ Bottleneck: **Email #3** (High Drop-off)

The dashboard also features a navigation menu on the left side with options for **Overview**, **Reports**, **Funnels**, **Goals**, and **Config**. A button at the bottom reads **"Track what matters"**. Overall, the dashboard provides a comprehensive overview of marketing performance, highlighting key metrics and areas for improvement.
